title: Imaging Lab Sequence Coordination
description: A scheduled-care coordination workflow that aligns appointments, dependencies, and staff/resource availability across clinical units. Focus case: imaging lab sequence coordination.
assumptions: Time is discretized into non-overlapping appointment slots; assignment removes slot availability.
Staff and device qualifications are pre-validated off-line; assignments assume credential compliance.
Laboratory and imaging resources report availability and are exclusive when reserved for a slot.
Clinical protocols fully specify required supplies (e.g., contrast) and staff roles for execution.
risks: Scheduling conflicts due to concurrent assignments; race conditions if availability is stale.
Resource failures (device outage, staff absence) that invalidate reservations mid-sequence.
Patient no-show or late arrival causing downstream lab specimen timing mismatches.
Communication delays between referring facility and coordination center resulting in incorrect protocol selection.
Consumable shortages (contrast agents) causing partial execution or last-minute cancellations.
